problem_addressed: Accurate EV power estimation underpins range prediction and energy management, yet practitioners need both point accuracy and trustworthy uncertainty quantification.
core_claim: An anchored-ensemble LSTM with Student-t likelihood jointly captures epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty, delivering accurate, well-calibrated predictions with efficient real-time inference.
key_contributions: Anchored ensembles impose Gaussian weight priors via MAP training to generate posterior-like diversity without test-time sampling
Student-t likelihood provides heavy-tailed robustness and closed-form prediction intervals for aleatoric uncertainty
Achieves strong accuracy (RMSE 3.36±1.10, MAE 2.21±0.89, R²=0.93±0.02) and well-calibrated uncertainty bands with near-nominal coverage
novelty: Combines anchored weight priors (MAP training) with Student-t likelihood in an ensemble LSTM to eliminate Monte Carlo latency while maintaining Bayesian uncertainty quantification and robustness to heavy-tailed residuals.
method_summary: Trains multiple LSTM models with gate-wise Gaussian priors anchored to learned weights, using Student-t negative log-likelihood for aleatoric uncertainty. Predictive intervals combine ensemble spread (epistemic) and t-distribution scale (aleatoric).
method_components: Anchored ensemble LSTM with gate-wise Gaussian priors (MAP objective)
Student-t likelihood for heteroscedastic uncertainty estimation
Closed-form predictive intervals using ensemble averaging and t-distribution quantiles
key_innovation: Anchoring imposes Gaussian weight priors during MAP training, generating posterior-like diversity without test-time sampling, enabling efficient uncertainty quantification.
assumptions: Input features are normalized to [0,1] range
Residuals follow Student-t distribution with ν>2 for finite variance
Gate-wise factorization of prior and likelihood enables independent quadratic penalties
theoretical_grounding: Proposition proves MAP optimality under sample-factorized likelihood and block-factorized prior (Appendix 7). Student-t variance defined as Var[Y|x] = ν/(ν-2)s(x)² for ν>2.
headline_result: t-NLL Anchor achieved best calibration with standardized variance near unity (≈1.13) for Sedan and IONIQ 5 datasets
improvement_over_baseline: t-NLL Anchor showed better calibration than t-NLL Dropout (StVar 1.13 vs 0.948 for Sedan) and avoided under-confidence issues of Quantile methods (StVar 0.11 for IONIQ 5)
stated_limitations: The model is specifically designed for EV power consumption prediction using vehicle-kinematic time series, which might limit its applicability to other domains.
The model might be computationally intensive due to the ensemble approach.
unstated_weaknesses: The abstract doesn't mention the computational requirements or training time.
It's unclear how the model performs with limited data or in scenarios with high noise.
The paper doesn't discuss how the model handles concept drift or changes in driving patterns over time.
threats_to_validity: The model's performance might be specific to the type of vehicles and driving conditions in the dataset.
The generalizability to different EV models or driving environments is not addressed.
The paper doesn't discuss the impact of sensor noise or data quality on model performance.
open_questions: How does the anchored-ensemble approach compare to other Bayesian methods for uncertainty quantification?
What is the optimal size of the ensemble for this task?
How does the model perform with different types of time series data or in other domains?
future_work: Extending the model to other domains beyond EV power consumption.
Exploring more efficient ensemble methods or alternative uncertainty quantification techniques.
Investigating the model's performance with real-time data streams.
gaps_this_enables: Applying Bayesian uncertainty quantification techniques to time series data in other domains.
Combining ensemble methods with probabilistic modeling for uncertainty quantification.
Developing models that can provide both point predictions and uncertainty estimates.
relevance_to_topic: This paper is relevant to calibration-aware active learning for medical image classification as it provides a framework for uncertainty quantification, which is a key component of active learning.
reusable_assets: The Student-t likelihood approach for handling heavy-tailed distributions.
The methodology for combining MAP training with ensemble diversity.
best_result: t-NLL Anchor achieved best calibration with standardized variance near unity (≈1.13) for Sedan and IONIQ 5 datasets
limitations_stated: The model is specifically designed for EV power consumption prediction using vehicle-kinematic time series, which might limit its applicability to other domains.
The model might be computationally intensive due to the ensemble approach.
title: Bayesian Uncertainty Quantification with Anchored Ensembles for Robust EV Power Consumption Prediction
